selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 0.3% and 20-day momentum is 8.3%.
Trend quality is supported by 60% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 5.6% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.6%, downside volatility near 1.3%, and a recent max drawdown of 3.3%.
Volume confirmation is 8.6% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 2.8% versus SPY over 5 days and 1.6% versus the sector ETF.
No recent relevant news passed the filter, so the news layer stayed neutral and contributed less confidence.
Recent JPM company-news coverage was mostly broad market roundups or weakly direct mentions, so the company-news contribution was forced back to neutral.
Broad market news leaned bullish for the Financials sector with score 0.60 and confidence 0.36.

Valley National reports an improving asset mix and a secure dividend, implying higher net interest margins and/or lower credit risk for the bank. First-order effect: VLY-specific profitability and dividend stability could lead to modest upward re-rating of the stock and lift sentiment for regional banks. Second-order effect: slightly improved sentiment for the financials sector could support short-term flows into bank stocks. Impact is narrow (regional bank) and not company-specific to tracked symbol JPM, so only the financials sector is listed as a modest beneficiary. Overall effect is small and short-horizon given the company-specific nature.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Overall model score is 0.048 with low confidence.
